about.title: Building a more compassionate Jordan for all animals
about.whyDesc1: Tabanni (تبنّي) which means "Adoption" in Arabic, was born from a simple realization: Jordan has thousands of loving animals looking for homes, and just as many compassionate people looking for pets. The missing link was a centralized, easy-to-use platform to connect them.
about.whyDesc2: Our mission is to end animal homelessness in Jordan by making adoption the first choice, promoting fostering as a crucial stepping stone, and educating the public about animal welfare.
about.vettedPetsDesc: All listed pets are screened for health and behavior.
about.communityDrivenDesc: Powered by volunteers, rescuers, and animal lovers.
about.getInTouchSub: Have questions about adoption? Want to partner with us? We'd love to hear from you.
about.ourTeamSub: Meet the passionate people behind Tabanni who work every day to give animals a second chance.
about.programsSub: Structured initiatives that help us make adoption and fostering accessible for everyone in Jordan.
about.programAdoptDesc: Connecting vetted pets with loving forever homes through a safe and guided adoption process.
about.programFosterDesc: Temporary care placements that help animals recover and prepare for permanent adoption.
about.programEducationDesc: Workshops and resources that promote responsible pet ownership and animal welfare.
about.programVetDesc: Partnering with clinics to provide affordable veterinary care for rescue animals.
about.networkDesc: Tabanni operates across multiple cities in Jordan, working with local shelters, vet clinics, and community volunteers to reach every corner of the kingdom.
about.sustainabilityDesc: Our long-term vision is a Jordan where no animal goes without care — built on partnerships, education, and community responsibility.
about.sus1: Partnering with businesses to create sustainable funding streams for animal welfare.
about.sus2: Training local communities in humane animal handling and care.
about.sus3: Advocating for stronger animal protection legislation in Jordan.
about.faqsSub: Everything you need to know about adopting, fostering, and supporting animals through Tabanni.
about.faq1Q: How do I adopt a pet through Tabanni?
about.faq1A: Browse our Adopt page, find a pet you love, and submit an adoption request. Our team will review your application and guide you through the process.
about.faq2A: Fostering means providing temporary care for an animal in your home. We supply support and guidance — you provide the love and space.
about.faq3Q: Can I list my pet for adoption?
about.faq3A: Yes! Visit the Adopt page and click 'Add Your Pet for Adoption'. Fill in the details and our team will review and publish the listing.
about.faq4Q: How do my donations get used?
about.faq4A: All contributions go directly toward veterinary care, rescue operations, community education, and platform maintenance.
about.orgIntro: tabanni is a nonprofit organization dedicated to improving animal welfare in Jordan. Registered as The Jordanian Society for Animal Protection (JSAP) in April 2021, tabanni serves as its operational arm, focusing on rescue, rehabilitation, adoption, and awareness. Our name, tabanni — which means "adoption" in Arabic — reflects our mission of promoting responsible pet ownership and fostering a compassionate society for animals. The organization is primarily driven by a dedicated group of volunteers who contribute their time, energy, and love to help animals in need.
about.jsapName: The Jordanian Society for Animal Protection
about.visionText: A world where humans and animals coexist harmoniously. Where communities are united in their understanding of the vital role animals play in sustaining a healthy ecosystem.
about.missionText: Fostering a movement through education, advocacy and community engagement, where members of the community are proactive participants in adopting compassionate and sustainable practices towards animals.
about.whatWeDoBullet1Desc: We provide medical care and shelter for rescued animals.
about.whatWeDoBullet2Desc: We connect rescued pets with loving families.
about.whatWeDoBullet3Desc: We educate the public on animal welfare, ethical pet ownership, and the importance of adoption.
about.howWeOperateIntro: Unlike traditional shelters, tabanni operates through a network of foster homes. Instead, we rely on:
about.howWeOperateText1: and paid boarding for rescued animals.
about.howWeOperateText2: who help with rescues, adoption coordination, and awareness campaigns.
about.howWeOperateText3: through monetary contributions, fostering, and supplies.
about.ourImpactBullet1: Rescued and rehabilitated hundreds of animals
about.ourImpactBullet2: Facilitated the adoption of numerous dogs and cats into loving homes
about.ourImpactBullet4: Educated the community on responsible pet ownership
about.joinIntro: Tabanni operates solely on donations and community support.
about.joinText1: – Your donation helps save a life
about.joinText2: – Provide a temporary home for a rescued animal
about.joinText3: – Give a pet a second chance at a forever home
about.joinText4: – Help with rescues, events, or social media outreach

team.intro: tabanni is led by a group of five visionary board members. At its heart, our work is an all-volunteer workforce that has helped us rescue hundreds of animals since we started in 2019. Meet the team that makes our mission come to life every day, making a real difference for animals in Jordan.
team.roleGidaHamam: Director of Rescue Management and Placements
team.ctaText: We're saving lives one paw at a time, and we need your help. Whether through donations, fostering, adopting, or volunteering, every action makes a difference. Join us in creating a more compassionate world for animals.

network.partnerText: Our partners are carefully chosen to ensure they share and respect our high-value system, which revolves around respecting the basic rights of animals to co-exist harmoniously with us, humans. We strongly encourage people to adopt animals instead of buying them, as we believe that life, whether human or animal, should not be reduced to a price label. Moreover, we stand against pet shops that sell animals, particularly those who do so illegally without respecting the pets' basic needs for adequate space, sanitation, nutrition, affection, and movement. This is especially relevant in places like Canaan, where Animal Volunteer efforts help rescue dogs and promote responsible pet ownership.

faqs.faq1A: tabanni was registered as an NPO in April 2021 under The Jordanian Society for Animal Protection (JSAP). It has been active since 2019.
faqs.faq2A: tabanni is led by a team of volunteers and animal advocates who were active in animal welfare before officially registering the organization.
faqs.faq3A: tabanni promotes animal welfare, awareness, and education, focusing on rescue efforts and Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) programs to control stray populations humanely.
faqs.faq4A: No, tabanni does not operate a shelter but supports individual rescuers and partner organizations in rehoming rescued animals.
faqs.faq5A: Yes, tabanni relies entirely on donations from supporters. Contributions cover medical care, food, boarding, and rescue operations. Donate here →
faqs.faq6A: You can donate through bank transfer or in-person pickup. We also welcome volunteers and foster families to temporarily care for rescued pets. Donate or become a foster →
faqs.faq7A: 100% of donations go towards medical treatment, boarding, food, transportation, rehabilitation, and essential supplies.
faqs.faq8Q: Why does tabanni pay for boarding?
faqs.faq8A: Finding adopters and fosters is challenging. When no options are available, boarding is the only alternative to ensure animals remain safe until adoption.
faqs.faq9Q: Does tabanni help with reported rescues?
faqs.faq9A: Yes, we guide and support individuals who report animal rescues, encouraging community members to take action.
faqs.faq10Q: Are all adoption posts tabanni's rescues?
faqs.faq10A: No, some adoption posts are from external rescuers, and we help by promoting them to increase their chances of finding homes.
faqs.faq11Q: Can I post my pet for adoption on tabanni's platform?
faqs.faq11A: Yes, fill out our intake form, and we will share your pet's details as soon as possible.
faqs.faq12A: No, tabanni does not receive international funding and operates solely through local donations.
faqs.faq13Q: Does tabanni help with lost pets?
faqs.faq13A: Yes! We have a dedicated Instagram page for lost and found pets to help reunite them with their families.
faqs.faq14A: tabanni believes that animals deserve a dignified life and should not be treated as commercial products. We oppose illegal pet trade and advocate for ethical treatment.

programs.intro: From emergency rescues to long-term advocacy, tabanni runs a wide range of programs designed to protect and improve the lives of animals across Jordan.
programs.rescueDesc: tabanni rescues animals in need and provides them with immediate care, focusing on cases where the animal's life is in danger. tabanni deploys field officers to rescue animals and takes them to veterinary clinics for necessary medical care. After receiving emergency treatment, the animals join a rehab program and are eventually placed for adoption. Fundraising is an ongoing effort to ensure that financial resources are available for rescues. Our organization also supports individuals who call on us with emergency rescue cases.
programs.adoptionDesc: Our adoption program connects animals with loving forever homes across Jordan. Every animal in our care undergoes health screening and behavioral assessment before being matched with the right family. We guide adoptive families through the entire process — from application to homecoming — ensuring a smooth transition for both pet and owner. We believe every animal deserves a second chance at a happy life.
programs.rightsTitle: Fight for Animals Rights in Jordan
programs.rightsDesc: tabanni actively advocates for stronger animal welfare legislation and enforcement in Jordan. We work with policymakers, legal experts, and community leaders to push for meaningful change. Through awareness campaigns, petitions, and direct engagement with authorities, we shine a light on animal cruelty and push for accountability. Together, we are working toward a legal framework that protects animals and holds abusers responsible.
programs.strayCareDesc: Our Stray Care Program provides ongoing support for stray and community animals across Jordan. Through regular feeding stations, mobile veterinary visits, and community education, we improve the quality of life for animals living on the streets. We coordinate with local residents and municipalities to ensure that stray animals receive the compassionate treatment they deserve.
programs.strayCommunitiesDesc: Our Stray Dog Communities Program implements a Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) approach to manage and stabilize stray dog populations humanely. Dogs are trapped, neutered, vaccinated, and returned to their communities with ongoing monitoring and support. This evidence-based approach reduces overpopulation while maintaining the presence of community dogs that locals rely on and care for.
programs.rehabDesc: Animals rescued from the streets or difficult situations often need time, care, and patience before they are ready for adoption. Our Rehab Program provides a safe environment where animals receive medical treatment, nutritional support, behavioral training, and socialization. Our dedicated team of volunteers and foster families work hand-in-hand to help each animal reach their full potential.
programs.basboosDes: The Basboos Project is tabanni's dedicated initiative for street cats. Street cats face unique challenges including disease, malnutrition, and hazardous urban environments. Through targeted TNR campaigns, community feeding programs, and adoption drives, The Basboos Project works to create a safer, healthier life for Jordan's feline population. The name 'basboos' — the local term of endearment for cats — reflects the love and respect we have for every cat in our care.

lostFoundDetail.tipsLost: Tips to Help Find This Pet
lostFoundDetail.tipLost1: Share this post on your social media to spread the word.
lostFoundDetail.tipLost2: Check nearby shelters and veterinary clinics.
lostFoundDetail.tipLost3: Look in the area where the pet was last seen, especially early morning.
lostFoundDetail.tipLost4: Put out their favorite food or toys to attract them back.
lostFoundDetail.tipLost5: Contact local community groups in the area.
lostFoundDetail.tipFound1: Keep the pet in a safe, warm place away from other animals.
lostFoundDetail.tipFound2: Take the pet to a vet to check for a microchip.
lostFoundDetail.tipFound3: Post photos in local neighborhood groups online.
lostFoundDetail.tipFound4: Avoid giving the pet a new name to help reunification.
lostFoundDetail.tipFound5: Contact local shelters to report the found pet.
